evals.prompt: Implement a TaskDetail screen that loads a task from a remote API. Use MVI contracts and make expected loading failures visible to the UI.
evals.expected_output: A TDD-oriented plan or code sketch with TaskDetailUserIntent, TaskDetailUiState, TaskDetailSideEffect when needed, TaskDetailViewModel extending MVIViewModel, LoadTaskAction with suspend operator fun invoke returning Result, an optional repository because a remote API boundary exists, and ViewModel mapping Result success/failure to state or side effects without calling the repository directly.
assertions: The output creates or aligns UserIntent, UiState, SideEffect, and MVIViewModel contracts.
The ViewModel depends on LoadTaskAction or LoadTaskUseCase, not TaskRepository or a data source.
The action or use case defines suspend operator fun invoke.
Expected failures are returned as Result failures with typed errors.
Success maps to UiState content and recoverable failures map to UiState error or an appropriate SideEffect.
Tests cover both action/use case behavior and ViewModel state/effect behavior.

evals.prompt: Review a proposed MVI ViewModel that calls TaskRepository directly, catches exceptions with runCatching, and stores a snackbar message in UiState.
evals.expected_output: A review that flags the repository dependency, exception catching above the repository boundary, and one-shot snackbar stored in UiState. It should recommend an action or use case returning Result, persistent render state in UiState, snackbar/navigation as SideEffect, and tests for intent handling plus success/failure mapping.
assertions: The output says the ViewModel should not call the repository directly.
The output moves expected failures to Result from an action or use case.
The output says snackbar messages are one-shot SideEffects, not UiState.
The output keeps ViewModel intent handling exhaustive.
The output recommends focused ViewModel tests.
